message:
bd-3fc.8: Performance regression detection accuracy tests

Add 22 unit tests for regression detection accuracy:
- Known 15% regression is correctly detected
- Known 5% noise is NOT falsely flagged
- Boundary conditions at 10% threshold (just over/under)
- Performance improvements not misclassified
- Zero baseline division safety
- slo.yaml config parsing (thresholds, comments, defaults)
- SLO absolute threshold breach detection (max_p99_us)
- Per-metric regression ratio enforcement
- Unconfigured metrics fall back to global threshold
- Multi-metric batch comparison with mixed results
- Statistical noise profile (±5%) produces zero false positives
- Tracing: WARN for regressions, DEBUG for noise, TRACE for stable
- Ratio calculation correctness across various scenarios
- Custom threshold sensitivity (strict 3% vs relaxed 25%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Integration with FrankenLab seed-controlled scheduling. API: Lab::record(seed) -> Recording, Lab::replay(recording) -> Output, assert_deterministic(output1, output2). TRACING: span 'lab.record' and 'lab.replay' with fields. LOG: INFO for recording start/stop, WARN for divergence.  {"id":"bd-3fc1b","title":"task(graveyard-§7.6): Quotient Filters for dirty row tracking","description":"Use Quotient Filters as a space-efficient probabilistic data structure for tracking dirty rows in large virtualized lists. A Quotient Filter is a compact approximate membership structure (like a Bloom filter) but supports deletion and merging.

## Graveyard Entry

Section 7.6 -- Quotient Filters
Priority: 3

## Background and Math Foundations

Quotient Filters (Bender et al. 2012) store fingerprints in a hash table using quotienting:
- Hash each element to a p-bit fingerprint
- Split into q-bit quotient (slot index) and r-bit remainder (stored value)
- Use linear probing with run-length encoding for collisions
- Space: ~10% overhead above information-theoretic minimum
- Supports: insert, lookup, delete, merge, resize -- all O(1) amortized

For dirty row tracking:
- Insert row_id when row is modified
- Check membership to decide if row needs re-render
- Delete after re-render (Bloom filters cannot delete)
- Merge dirty sets from multiple update sources
- False positive rate: configurable (1% default -- skip re-render of ~1% clean rows)

Advantage over bitset: when list is very large (>1M rows) but dirty set is small (<1%), Quotient Filter uses O(dirty_count) space vs O(total_rows) for bitset.

## Structured Logging

All tests and benchmarks must emit structured JSONL evidence for evidence-ledger integration and CI artifact gates. Example records:
- op: qf_insert, row_id, fp_bits, load_factor, timestamp_ns
- op: qf_lookup, row_id, found, false_positive, probe_count
- op: qf_merge, source_count, result_size, fp_collisions

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Quotient Filter implementation with insert, lookup, delete, merge
2. False positive rate configurable and measured
3. Space comparison vs bitset at various dirty fractions
4. Property tests: no false negatives (all dirty rows detected)
5. Benchmark: lookup and insert latency comparison vs bitset and HashSet
6. Integration with virtualized list widget
7. All tests emit structured JSONL logs for evidence-ledger integration
8. CI artifacts: false-positive rate report, space comparison, latency benchmarks","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:51.370341055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:05.949924301Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","dirty-tracking","graveyard","quotient-filter"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fc1b","depends_on_id":"bd-3jlw5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
